Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0xA89A "MIGALLEYACE-4000-A89A" -Di_irq_exit=0 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 lda #$36 sta $01 jsr $4000 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Mig Alley Ace (J1).prg"
Metadata.Notes:
Added an init routine at $4000 which: 1. Turns off raster interrupts 2. Sets the correct border and screen colours 3. Sets the correct bitmap settings 4. Copies 1024 bytes of data from $4050 down to $0000 so that the dump can start at $0400 5. Sets the pointer to the current byte in the BASIC program ($7A/$7B) to $3D/$25 (the start of the title screen code) 6. Sets the stack pointer to $E2 7. And finally jumps to the entry point at $A89A (it couldn't use RTS because of the stack being modified)
Additionally, the following patches are applied to the dump:
1. $2ACB is set to $81 to disable the manual protection 2. $29A5/$29A6 are set to $80/$81 to give the full level of ammo 3. $1190, $1591, $1F8C, $1FA6 and $2876 are all set to $06 to remove disk drive check protection
Note: to dump the game, you should wait until the loading screen is hidden, followed by one short and six long flashes of the loading screen, then set a breakpoint at $A89A. This is because the game essentially implements it's own partial BASIC interpreter, presumably as an obfuscation tactic, so setting a simple breakpoint becomes harder.
